title: Cabled dishcloth
gaugeText: 20 sts × 28 rows = 10 × 10 cm in stocking stitch on 4.5 mm needles.
sources: Public-domain dishcloth pattern tradition — cabled dishcloths appear in the Weldon's series from the 1890s onward as 'fancy dishcloths'
Mary Thomas's Knitting Book (1938), §Cables (the c4f structure)
USDA / state-extension public-domain pamphlets on home-knit cotton dishcloths from the 1930s WPA era
notes: Chart-renderer exerciser. Ships a Tutorial-level `chartDefinition` showing a 4-row × 14-stitch cable repeat with c4f at the centre, framed by stocking-stitch panels and garter-stitch borders. The reader sees the cable in symbolic form alongside the written instructions. Cotton yarn so the finished cloth is functional. No blocking required for cotton — note that explicitly in Finishing.
